explanation: This property ensures that globally (G), if the system is in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) Mode and the inspiratory flow (V_E) drops below the Expiratory Trigger Setting (ETS) multiplied by the peak flow (PeakV_E), then the expiratory phase must immediately begin. This directly captures the requirement for the ventilator to stop providing pressure and allow exhalation upon reaching the trigger condition in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV mode (bool_exp1) AND the inspiratory flow (V_E) drops below the expiratory trigger setting (ETS) of a given breath (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the ventilator shall stop providing pressure (inValveClose) and allow exhalation (outValveOpen) (bool_exp3). This captures the immediate reactive behavior required by the natural language.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode) and the inspiratory flow drops below a fraction of the peak flow (V_E_lessThan_ExpiratoryTriggerSensitivity_multiplyBy_PeakV_E), then in the very next state (X), the inspiratory phase must end (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), the expiratory phase must start (expiratoryPhaseStart), the inspiratory valve must close (inValveClose), and the expiratory valve must open (outValveOpen). This captures the immediate reactive behavior required when the inspiratory flow condition is met in PSV mode.

The property asserts that globally, whenever the system is in PSV mode and the inspiratory flow drops below the specified expiratory trigger sensitivity, then in the very next state, the expiratory phase shall commence.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PSV Mode (bool_exp1) and the inspiratory flow drops below the expiratory trigger setting (bool_exp2), then the inspiratory valve must close and the expiratory valve must open (bool_exp3) simultaneously. This directly captures the conditional behavior: if the combined trigger condition is met, the specified action must occur immediately.

This property ensures that in the Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) Mode, when the inspiratory flow drops below the specified fraction of the peak flow (Expiratory Trigger Setting, ETS), the ventilator ceases pressure support and initiates the expiratory phase. The LTL template uses the 'rising edge' of the trigger condition to ensure the response happens when the condition is first met. Specifically, 'bool_exp2' represents the combined condition of being in PSV Mode and the inspiratory flow dropping below the threshold. 'bool_exp3' represents the desired outcome, which is the start of the expiratory phase. The first part of the formula, 'G (((! bool_exp2) & (X bool_exp2)) -> (X bool_exp3))', ensures that globally, if the condition represented by 'bool_exp2' becomes true (i.e., its rising edge), then 'bool_exp3' must be true in the very next state. The second part, '& (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)', ensures that whenever the trigger condition 'bool_exp2' holds, the consequence 'bool_exp3' also holds. This covers both the immediate reaction to the trigger's onset and its continued validity.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PSV mode (bool_exp1) and the inspiratory flow drops below the expiratory trigger sensitivity threshold (bool_exp2), then in the very next moment, the expiratory phase shall begin (bool_exp3). This ensures that the ventilator immediately stops providing pressure and allows exhalation upon detecting the specified flow condition in PSV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV Mode and the inspiratory flow drops below the expiratory trigger setting multiplied by the peak flow, then the inspiratory valve must close and the expiratory valve must open. This accurately captures the requirement for the ventilator to stop providing pressure and allow exhalation under the specified conditions.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (bool_exp1) AND the inspiratory flow drops below the expiratory trigger setting (bool_exp2), then the expiratory phase must start (bool_exp3). This directly captures the requirement for the ventilator to stop providing pressure and allow exhalation under the specified conditions in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV mode (bool_exp1) AND the inspiratory flow drops below the expiratory trigger sensitivity (bool_exp2) at any given moment, then in the very next state (X), the inspiratory valve shall close, the expiratory valve shall open, and the expiratory phase shall begin (bool_exp3). This precisely captures the requirement for the ventilator to stop providing pressure and allow exhalation immediately after the specified flow condition is met in PSV mode.
